macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin and Major Cryptos Tumble Amid Market Selloff
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly rallied above $70,000 but tumbled, down 1.9%, with Ether, Solana, and XRP also declining 2-2.6% as investors flee riskier assets amid Asia market meltdown and Iran concerns. Circle (USDC issuer) bucks the trend, up nearly 7-24%, while mining stocks like MicroStrategy, Riot Platforms slide at least 2%.[1]

items.title: Trump Pressures Banks to Advance Crypto Market Structure Bill
items.summary: President Trump urges banks and Congress to pass crypto legislation like the Clarity Act ASAP, accusing Wall Street of hindering digital asset innovation and pushing a 'Crypto Agenda' to keep it in the US. Markets reacted positively initially but pulled back amid uncertainty.[2][3][4]

items.title: BlackRock Bitcoin ETF Accumulates $264M Despite Volatility
items.summary: BlackRock's spot Bitcoin ETF aggressively accumulated $264 million worth of Bitcoin amid market uncertainty, signaling strong institutional buying even as prices dip.[3]

items.title: Trump-Affiliated American Bitcoin Company Expands Mining 12%
items.summary: The American Bitcoin Company, linked to Trump, is expanding mining capacity by 12% with a pure mining and hoarding strategy amid broader market declines.[1]

items.title: Ripple Expands XRP in Payments and Stablecoin Hits Top 50,000
items.summary: Ripple advances XRP use in payments with stablecoin exploding into the top 50,000; high ETF inflows and buys from MicroStrategy, Bitmine, and Ark persist despite dips.[2]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 Solves Long-Standing Computer Science Problem on Directed Hamiltonian Cycles
items.summary: Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.6, released three weeks ago, solved a longstanding computer science problem on decomposing complete graphs into Hamiltonian cycles, impressing Stanford's Don Knuth who conceded the need to revise his views on generative AI. The AI used advanced deduction techniques after initial heuristic failures.

items.title: Google Consolidates Industrial Robotics AI with Intrinsic Acquisition and Boston Dynamics Integration
items.summary: Google officially absorbs Intrinsic's robotics platform, integrating it with DeepMind AI models, Gemini for Atlas robots, and Google Cloud to target the $370B industrial robotics market by 2040. This enables better sensor interpretation, adaptation, and complex task execution in manufacturing.

items.title: AI Advances in Energy Materials Discovery: From ML to Large Models for Batteries and Catalysts
items.summary: Tongji University researchers outline AI's evolution in energy materials, using large models to predict battery lifespans, optimize electrolytes, and design catalysts for green hydrogen and CO2 reduction. Future includes self-driving laboratories despite data quality challenges.

items.title: Alibaba Qwen AI Team Lead Junyang Lin Departs After Major Model Launch
items.summary: Junyang Lin, tech lead for Alibaba’s Qwen AI team, has left following a significant model release, highlighting high stakes and leadership shifts in AI research amid funding frenzies and ethical concerns in software development.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo Indie World March 2026 Showcase Reveals Multiple Switch Titles
items.summary: Nintendo's Indie World on March 3 announced games like Denshattack (June 17 on Switch 2), Rotwood (today, Switch 2 exclusive), Minishoot' Adventures (today on Switch/Switch 2), and others including Ratatan (July 16), Blue Prince (today), and more launching in spring/summer 2026.[2]

items.title: Xbox Game Pass March 2026 Adds Cyberpunk 2077 and Planet of Lana 2
items.summary: Xbox Game Pass is adding Cyberpunk 2077, Planet of Lana 2, To a T, and other titles in March 2026 wave one.[4]

items.title: US Trump Administration Debates Curbing Tencent Stakes in Riot and Epic Games
items.summary: The Trump administration is debating forced divestment of Tencent's stakes in Riot Games (100%) and Epic Games (28%), citing national security concerns over data, amid upcoming Trump-Xi talks.[5][6]

items.title: Capcom Spotlight and Bandai Namco RPG Teased for March 5
items.summary: Capcom's Spotlight on March 5 highlights Street Fighter 6, Monster Hunter Stories, with Resident Evil 9 possibly teased; Bandai Namco teases new RPG reveal same day.[3]

items.title: GDC 2026 Preview: GTA 6 Trends, AI-Driven Hardware Shortages, Steam Machine Delay
items.summary: Upcoming GDC 2026 to discuss GTA 6 influence, Nintendo Switch 2 ports, AI-induced RAM/HDD shortages delaying Valve's Steam Machine and impacting PS6, plus Steam Next Fest buzz.[1]

items.title: Xbox Leadership Reaffirms Console Focus Amid Fan Pushback
items.summary: Xbox head emphasizes refocus on console business in all-hands meeting, as fans send clear message amid industry shifts and calls for more exclusives.[3]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Launches New MacBook Air with M5 Chip and MacBook Pro Refresh
items.summary: Apple unveiled a new MacBook Air featuring the M5 chip, up to four times faster at AI tasks than the M4, alongside MacBook Pro models with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips that are $100-$400 more expensive due to RAM shortages. Additional launches include a cheaper iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air with improved performance and connectivity.

items.title: NVIDIA Invests $4B in Photonics Suppliers for AI Data Center Scaling
items.summary: NVIDIA announced $2 billion investments each in Lumentum and Coherent, with multiyear commitments to secure optical components essential for high-speed data movement in AI data centers. This move aims to control the supply chain for AI infrastructure beyond just accelerators.

items.title: Nvidia-Backed Ayar Labs Raises $500M for Light-Based AI Chip Interconnects
items.summary: Ayar Labs, supported by NVIDIA, secured $500 million to advance optical interconnects that speed up AI chips using light-based technology. The funding targets improvements in AI networking performance.

items.title: Mobile World Congress 2026 Kicks Off with Robot Phones and AI PCs Announcements
items.summary: MWC 2026 opened in Barcelona on March 2, featuring announcements of robot phones and AI PCs among telecom and consumer tech reveals, with the event running through March 5.

items.title: NVIDIA Ranks as Fastest-Growing Tech Brand, Top 100 Brands Worth $3.7 Trillion
items.summary: The world's top 100 tech brands reached $3.7 trillion in value, with nearly 70% in the top four US brands; NVIDIA rose to fifth place after more than doubling its value since 2025.
